meaning: adj.虚弱的,脆弱的,(意志)薄弱的n.<美俚>少女,少妇,灯心草篓
examples: She lay in bed looking frail.
My grandmother was becoming more and more sad and frail as the years went by.
The issue is not whether human knowledge is frail, but whether it is disbursed.
He still looks somewhat frail, relying on a cane and using a wheelchair and walker occasionally.
